response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and has demonstrated low-latency distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement is modern production-grade C++ or Rust — neither of which appears in his active skillset. His C++ use is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; no Rust is mentioned anywhere. His primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang, and his geospatial work is web-GIS data collection rather than routing tile pipelines or navigation guidance systems, making the technical gap too wide for a mid-senior C++ hire.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application development
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, real-time metadata)
Broad cross-stack versatility and a track record of picking up unfamiliar problem domains independently
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015); zero Rust experience anywhere on the resume
Active language stack (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js) is entirely disconnected from the C++/Rust requirement that defines this role
No experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance pipelines, or geospatial data freshness/latency optimization at production scale
